commit.message:
fix: add /claims endpoint and fix wizard Thymeleaf expression

Two bugs fixed:

1. Missing GET /debts/claims endpoint (debt-service)
   The creditor portal called /debt-service/api/v1/debts/claims for all
   claim list views (recovery, hearing, zero-balance, rejected). The endpoint
   did not exist; Spring routed 'claims' to @GetMapping(/{id}), causing
   Invalid UUID string: claims and opening the circuit breaker.

   Added:
   - CreditorClaimListItemDto: superset of ClaimListItemDto and
     HearingClaimListItemDto so both list views deserialize correctly
   - DebtRepository.findClaimsForCreditor(): filters by creditorOrgId,
     lifecycleState (mapped from status param), and zero-balance flag
   - DebtService.getClaimsForCreditor() interface + DebtServiceImpl impl
   - DebtController: GET /claims before /{id} to avoid ambiguity

   Status mapping: IN_RECOVERY->OVERDRAGET, HEARING->HOERING,
   REJECTED->TILBAGEKALDT, ZERO_BALANCE->outstandingBalance=0

2. Thymeleaf 500 on opret fordring wizard (creditor-portal)
   The step indicator in all four wizard templates used 'step < currentStep'
   in a th:classappend expression. The < character is invalid in HTML attribute
   values and caused a TemplateProcessingException. Fixed by replacing < with
   the Thymeleaf comparison alias 'lt' in all four step-*.html templates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply suggestion from @Copilot

async Task, bitmap caching, filter composition logic

commit.message:
fix: include public/_pagefind/** in turbo build outputs to restore search index on cache hit (#1931) Search on suspensive.org was broken (`Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: /_pagefind/pagefind.js`) because Turbo's remote cache wasn't aware of the pagefind output directory — on cache hits, it was never restored. The docs build script generates the search index into `public/_pagefind/`: ``` pagefind --site .next/server/app --output-path public/_pagefind ``` But `turbo.json` only declared `dist/**` and `.next/**` as outputs, so `public/_pagefind/**` was silently dropped on cache restoration. ## Change - **`turbo.json`** — add `public/_pagefind/**` to the `build` task `outputs`: ```diff - "outputs": ["dist/**", ".next/**"] + "outputs": ["dist/**", ".next/**", "public/_pagefind/**"] ```
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > [Bug]: Failed to load search index (pagefind.js not found) > ### Package Scope > > etc > > ### Bug description > > The search functionality on suspensive.org is broken. > When typing a search query, the following error appears: > > ``` > > Failed to load search index. > > TypeError: Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: https://suspensive.org/pagefind/pagefind.js > ``` > > > > The request to https://suspensive.org/_pagefind/pagefind.js returns a 500 Internal Server Error, while the file is correctly generated locally after running `pnpm run build`. > > According to a commit from two days ago(https://github.com/toss/suspensive/pull/1929), I found the Next.js version was upgraded and the build script was modified along with it. Could this be related to the Turbo remote cache? > > ### Expected behavior > > Matching search results should be displayed, or a "no results" message should be shown if there are no matches. > > ### To Reproduce > > 1. Go to https://suspensive.org > 2. Type anything in the search input > 3. Get the error: "Failed to load search index" > > ### Possible Solution > > I was considering adding `"public/_pagefind/**"` to the outputs in `turbo.json`, since it might not currently cached or restored when Turbo remote cache hits. But since I can't verify your Vercel build configuration, I'm filing this as an issue first. > > ### etc. > > _No response_ > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >
- Fixes toss/suspensive#1930 --- ⌨️ Start Copilot coding agent tasks without leaving your editor — available in [VS Code](https://gh.io/cca-vs-code-docs), [Visual Studio](https://gh.io/cca-visual-studio-docs), [JetBrains IDEs](https://gh.io/cca-jetbrains-docs) and [Eclipse](https://gh.io/cca-eclipse-docs). --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Jonghyeon Ko Co-authored-by: constmoon <19185307+constmoon@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix critical security and logic bugs found in code review

Security Fixes:
- CRITICAL: Remove eval() for BUILD_CMD and TEST_CMD in patch_test.sh
  Replaced with bash -c to prevent command injection via env variables
- HIGH: Replace /tmp/build.log and /tmp/test.log with mktemp()
  Prevents race conditions and data corruption in multi-user systems

Logic & Validation Fixes:
- HIGH: Fix division by zero vulnerability in patch_check.sh calc_similarity()
  Added proper handling for zero adds/dels cases
- MEDIUM: Add input validation for silicon test results in patch_test.sh
  Only accepts PASS/FAIL/PENDING, defaults to PENDING on invalid input
- MEDIUM: Fix per-file diff stat calculation in patch_check.sh
  Use awk to track adds/dels per file instead of counting entire patch
- MEDIUM: Add validation for empty review branch in patch_check.sh
  Dies with clear error if branch name cannot be extracted from JSON
- LINUX-ONLY: Simplified stat command to use only Linux syntax (macOS support deferred)

All Python tests still pass (29/29 ✅)
All fixes tested for correctness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add bash versions of patch pipeline scripts

- patch_receive.sh: Validate and stage patches from a directory
- patch_apply.sh: Apply staged patches to a review branch
- patch_check.sh: Compare sender intent vs actual changes landed
- patch_test.sh: Run build and unit tests, collect silicon results
- patch_integrate.sh: Cherry-pick reviewed patches to working branch

All bash scripts:
- Provide zero-dependency alternatives to Python versions
- Generate compatible JSON output for downstream steps
- Support same configuration via .patch-pipeline.toml and env vars
- Can be mixed with Python scripts in the same pipeline
- Include comprehensive error handling and user-friendly output

Added BASH_README.md with detailed usage guide and examples.
